data.text/html:
Interactive paper implementation · move the mathematics, inspect the evidence

COMPRESSION FOR A FROZEN MACHINE

The Witness Machine

What is the smallest video that makes a frozen driving machine reach the same decision?

Interactive implementation and extension of DNN-Driven Compressive Offloading for Edge-Assisted Semantic Video Segmentation .

STAC asks where compression hurts. The Witness Machine asks which compact geometric coordinate should carry that debt. This challenge preserves a semantic partition, a two-frame motion signal, and a counted archive—not every pixel uniformly. Move one control and watch the representation follow the receiver.

2.7%of pixels are decision boundary
99.9%of the sensitivity mass sits there

Headline result, measured on one locked real comma.ai pair through the frozen SegNet · [macOS-CPU advisory]

THE REAL FRAME

Where a frozen machine actually looks

This is the locked real pair from the challenge video, at the frozen scorer's own working resolution. The frozen SegNet cuts the frame into five regions, and nearly all of its sensitivity presses against the thin boundary between them — the separatrix. Everything this notebook builds happens on this road.

[EMPIRICAL][macOS-CPU advisory][one fixed preselected pair] · the photographic panels are display substrates; every measured field and number comes from the sealed evidence artifact

The task is not to keep the pixels. It is to keep the boundary the machine reads.
5-MINUTE PATH real evidence→paper experiment→move the wall→stress the proxy→conclusion

REAL FROZEN-SCORER EVIDENCE

One frozen scorer, two meanings of sensitivity

On one fixed real comma.ai pair, the raw top-two-margin input gradient is broad. At the declared 1e-6 regularizer, normalization by the squared local decision margin makes a heuristic sensitivity proxy collapse toward the semantic wall. The result is regularizer- and logit-scale-sensitive, and it is not a calibrated flip probability because the summed-output gradient couples receptive fields.

smargin(x) = ‖∇xΣpm(p)‖² / (m(x)² + ε)
88.89%raw gradient mass outside a 3 px boundary annulus
99.89%margin-normalized sensitivity-proxy mass on the exact boundary

[EMPIRICAL][macOS-CPU advisory][one fixed preselected pair] · pixel-space summed-margin sensitivity proxy at epsilon=1e-6 · parameter-sensitive · no score · not STAC DCT sensitivity

The same real surface rejects edge-only saliency and shows how a declared small-margin heuristic can concentrate on a boundary at one fixed regularizer. It does not predict a flip probability or define a scale-invariant statistic.

ACROSS TRAINING

The texture is alien; the reading is right

The witness never tries to look like the road. It paints whatever texture makes the frozen scorer read the same partition — and across the training stages of one real n600 run, the realized reading converges onto the sealed target, lane markings included. The top row is what the witness paints; the bottom row is what the frozen scorer reads from it.

[EMPIRICAL][macOS-MLX training-gradient]/[macOS-CPU advisory][one locked pair] · per-stage EMA checkpoints of one n600 training run · no score, promotion, or exact-eval claim; the disagreement shown is this single pair's realized fraction, not the run-level d_seg

Compression for a machine optimizes the machine's reading, not the human's picture.

The paper controller spends this declared local budget across coefficients. Larger B permits coarser regional tables; it is not a measured task loss or score.

[TOY][DERIVATION] START HERE · Move B. One reactive state recomputes the receiver balance, the paper-controller table allocation, and the final carrier bids. Follow the same pressure from equation to witness.

Light, dark, color-vision-safe, monochrome, and motion preferences change presentation only; the computed geometry is invariant.

01 · FROZEN RECEIVER

One receiver, three debts

Segmentation disagreement is linear, pose disagreement enters through a square root, and every archive byte pays rate. The equation is official; the dial is an explorable design question, not a candidate result.

A byte has value only through the decision it protects.

At ρ=1 the outline-only policy is uniform. Larger ρ spends more of the same declared precision proxy on the semantic outline and less away from it.

[TOY][DERIVATION] Try ρ=1, then ρ=8. At matched Σ1/q, does outline-only still beat uniform?

02 · PAPER CONTROLLER

The network, not the outline, sets the price

STAC differentiates a frozen segmentation loss with respect to transform coefficients. Its first-order bound assigns smaller quantization steps where the loss is more sensitive, then approximates that ideal with regional table choices (§III, Eq. 2 and Algorithm 1; §IV, Algorithm 2).

ΔQmax = Σ |gi| qi / 2 ≤ B
qi* = 2B / (M |gi|)
declared B
12.000
realized bound
9.917
remaining slack
2.083
finite-bank gate
PASS · ≤ B

This first view is an analytic sensitivity field passed through the implemented STAC allocation kernel. It demonstrates the controller; it is not a SegNet backward pass.

Matched-effort counterexample: uniform, outline-only, and sensitivity-projected policies
policy first-order bound matched Σ1/q
uniform29.085224.00
outline-only33.406124.00
sensitivity-projected25.356824.00

At this field and matched proxy, outline-only loses to uniform.

TOY · same analytic field and matched reciprocal-step precision proxy · not JPEG · not an evaluated score

A boundary is a powerful coordinate, but frozen-network sensitivity can also live away from it. The residual is part of the model, not an embarrassment.

REAL TEMPORAL EVIDENCE

Transport the strategy; measure when topology refuses

A corrected custody pass uses true consecutive raw frames 392→393. Frozen SegNet partitions and a compact three-level strategy are forward-warped; holes, collisions, and disagreement stay visible instead of being painted over.

[ADVISORY] One downsampled Farneback image-flow construction. Not PoseNet, not STAC's DIS flow or refresh rule, and not an exact or proxy challenge score.

Transport the compact cause; refresh where receiver support or topology breaks.

<marimo-ui-element object-id='Kclp-0' random-id='9c2a9990-cd76-c52d-fe1a-d54eba0dd537'><marimo-slider data-initial-value='0.45' data-label='"\u003cspan class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"paragraph\"\u003eAppearance retained\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e"' data-start='0.0' data-stop='1.0' data-step='0.05' data-steps='[]' data-debounce='false' data-disabled='false' data-orientation='"horizontal"' data-show-value='true' data-include-input='false' data-full-width='true'></marimo-slider></marimo-ui-element>

03 · EQUIVALENCE CLASS

Preserve the answer, not the appearance

A frozen evaluator partitions videos into cells. Inside one cell, color and texture may change without changing the receiver. A small boundary crossing can leave the cell.

[V]F = { V̂ : F(R(V̂)) = F(R(V)) }

A witness is one compact representative of a frozen-output equivalence class.
Move one analytic circular SDF zero set. The partition, swept annulus, exact area, and first variation use the same normal displacement.

04 · BOUNDARY LENS

A moving decision wall sweeps task area

A signed-distance field stores a region by its zero set and normal direction. For a small normal displacement with no topological event, disagreement is the thin strip swept by that wall.

φ(x)=±dist(x,Γ),   Γ={φ=0}
∂tφ + vn‖∇φ‖ = 0
|Ω Δ Ω̂| ≈ ∫Γ|δn(s)| ds

Class changes begin in area swept by a decision boundary; births and merges need more.

<marimo-ui-element object-id='iLit-0' random-id='69feba09-244f-bf60-4869-10c3c0967fe0'><marimo-slider data-initial-value='0.25' data-label='"\u003cspan class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"paragraph\"\u003eSoft-max temperature\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e"' data-start='0.05' data-stop='1.2' data-step='0.05' data-steps='[]' data-debounce='false' data-disabled='false' data-orientation='"horizontal"' data-show-value='true' data-include-input='false' data-full-width='true'></marimo-slider></marimo-ui-element>

05 · TOPOLOGY

From a soft tie to a literal separatrix

Cooling log-sum-exp reveals a max-plus partition. Separately, the explicit potential U(x,y)=(x²−1)²+y² has two minima and one saddle: its stable manifold is literally a Morse–Smale separatrix. A class tie earns that name only when this dynamical condition holds.

τ log Σc exp(qc/τ) → maxc qc
ẋ = −∇U(x)

Topology tells the generator when a wall may move—and when a new component must be born.

06 · GENERATOR LENS

Store causes, not paint

Equal-curvature quadratic fields form a genuine Laguerre partition. Changing one additive weight moves a shared wall without repainting every pixel.

The exact power-diagram construction is a clean model; learned SDF walls may curve and require residual correction.

07 · SHARED CARRIERS

One scene, several irreducible coordinates

v8 is not five naive independent class fields. It is a heterogeneous generator-native program: Road↔Undrivable is strictly edge-centric; the lane band, movable-site carrier, hood model, and tiny tail edges keep their smallest natural coordinates before merge, diff, and correction.

Generate the horizon, lane band, moving sites, and hood once; merge, diff, correct.

08 · TEMPORAL APERTURE

A perfect partition can still starve motion

STAC transports a cached segmentation and regional strategy through dense flow, then refreshes when the propagated state becomes stale. Our geometry can carry the wall; two-frame perception still needs observable texture and occlusion repair.

Ẋ = ξ̂X,   ξ ∈ 𝔰𝔢(3)
T(θ)=exp(ξ̂θ)   — screw transport model

model coordinates are not asserted PoseNet semantics

Transport structure, preserve an aperture, and refresh at events the warp cannot explain.

09 · SHADOW-PRICE CONTROL

Let local shadow prices rank the next carrier

The score law supplies exact local derivatives with respect to its declared debts. A model then estimates how one counted unit changes each debt. Their product ranks boundary, motion, generator, and correction actions at this operating point.

λseg=100,   λpose=5/√(10dpose) for dpose>0,   λbyte=25/N
ΔSdrop(1)=λsegrseg+λposerpose−λbyteΔB,   𝒱=ΔSdrop(1)/ΔB

[TOY] Explicit hypothetical reductions at d_pose=0.02; every row still owes a real carrier and receiver round trip.

Declared carrier reductions and first-order score-drop proxy
carrier rseg rpose ΔB ΔSdrop(1)
boundary0.002140.0000428,000+0.1961
motion0.000250.0005738,000+0.0061
generator0.001210.0003064,000+0.0822
correction0.000670.0001522,000+0.0543

Edges are a compact basis; a declared local shadow-price proxy asks whether they earn the next byte.

Stress the proxy where it is most fragile

An optional batched Torch CUDA, MPS, or CPU sweep recomputes the declared margin-normalized sensitivity proxy on the locked real one-pair evidence across 256 regularizers and checks a deterministic slice against NumPy. It measures robustness of an advisory proxy, not STAC's DCT gradient or a challenge score.

[EXTERNAL] In Molab, attach a GPU from notebook specs. [ADVISORY] Without one, the same sweep reports CPU; the complete story remains available on NumPy.

10 · WHAT FELL OUT

What the notebook establishes

STAC supplies the local price; the extension proposes the compact coordinate that should carry it. Geometry generates a candidate witness, but the frozen receiver still decides what survives the round trip.

FROZEN SENSITIVITY → SHARED GEOMETRIC CARRIERS
→ TEXTURE / POSE / REPAIR → ROUND TRIP R
→ FROZEN SEGMENTATION + TWO-FRAME MOTION RECEIVER

[TOY][DERIVATION] At matched proxy effort, outline-only can lose to uniform on the declared analytic field.

[EMPIRICAL][macOS-CPU advisory][one locked pair] The margin-normalized frozen-scorer proxy changes materially across the declared ε range.

[TOY] Edge carriers and local shadow prices are a generator proposal, not an exact score result.

PROVENANCE · v7.5 shared trunk → v8 generator-native heterogeneous decomposition

Compress the evidence the frozen judge needs, and let geometry regenerate the rest.

      :root {
        color-scheme: light;
        --wm-bg: #f7f7f3;
        --wm-panel: #ffffff;
        --wm-panel-2: #cbd2d8;
        --wm-text: #17191c;
        --wm-muted: #4f5963;
        --wm-cyan: #006f8a;
        --wm-gold: #8a5a00;
        --wm-coral: #b73a37;
        --wm-mint: #527a00;
        --wm-violet: #5d4a9e;
        --wm-dark-ink: #101214;
        --wm-rule: color-mix(in srgb, var(--wm-muted) 26%, transparent);
      }

      @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
        :root {
          color-scheme: dark;
          --wm-bg: #0b0d10;
          --wm-panel: #11161c;
          --wm-panel-2: #2a333d;
          --wm-text: #f5f7f2;
          --wm-muted: #b7c0c9;
          --wm-cyan: #8bd5ff;
          --wm-gold: #ffd166;
          --wm-coral: #ff746c;
          --wm-mint: #b8f26a;
          --wm-violet: #b9a7ff;
          --wm-dark-ink: #0b0d10;
        }
      }

      body, marimo-app, marimo-app .app {
        background: var(--wm-bg) !important;
      }

      .wm-shell {
        width: min(100%, 1220px);
        margin-inline: auto;
        padding-inline: clamp(1rem, 3vw, 2.5rem);
        color: var(--wm-text);
        font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "SF Pro Text", Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif;
      }

      .wm-hero-grid,
      .wm-scene-grid {
        display: grid;
        grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) minmax(18rem, 1fr);
        align-items: center;
        gap: clamp(1.2rem, 4vw, 4rem);
        min-width: 0;
      }

      .wm-scene-grid {
        grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 0.78fr) minmax(20rem, 1.22fr);
      }

      .wm-copy,
      .wm-visual {
        min-width: 0;
      }

      .wm-visual > svg {
        width: 100%;
        max-width: 100%;
        height: auto;
      }

      .wm-proposal-table {
        width: 100%;
        margin-top: 1rem;
        border-collapse: collapse;
        font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
        font-size: clamp(0.7rem, 1.3vw, 0.84rem);
      }

      .wm-proposal-table th,
      .wm-proposal-table td {
        padding: 0.48rem 0.38rem;
        border-bottom: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--wm-muted) 24%, transparent);
        text-align: right;
      }

      .wm-proposal-table th:first-child,
      .wm-proposal-table td:first-child {
        text-align: left;
      }

      .wm-proposal-table caption {
        margin-block-end: 0.55rem;
        color: var(--wm-muted);
        font-size: 0.82rem;
        line-height: 1.4;
        text-align: start;
      }


      .wm-kicker {
        margin: 0 0 0.75rem;
        color: var(--wm-mint);
        font-family: "SFMono-Regular", Consolas, ui-monospace, monospace;
        font-size: 0.74rem;
        font-weight: 700;
        letter-spacing: 0.16em;
      }

      .wm-title {
        margin: 0;
        max-width: 12ch;
        color: var(--wm-text);
        font-size: clamp(3rem, 6.8vw, 5.9rem);
        line-height: 0.94;
        letter-spacing: -0.065em;
      }

      .wm-subtitle {
        max-width: 27ch;
        margin-block: 1.25rem 1rem;
        color: var(--wm-text);
        font-size: clamp(1.25rem, 2.3vw, 2rem);
        font-weight: 620;
        line-height: 1.2;
        letter-spacing: -0.025em;
      }

      .wm-paper,
      .wm-body {
        max-width: 64ch;
        color: var(--wm-muted);

        line-height: 1.62;
      }

        padding-inline-start: 0.9rem;
        border-inline-start: 3px solid var(--wm-gold);

      }

      }

        margin-block-start: 1.25rem;

      }

      }

      .wm-scope {

        font-size: 0.9rem;

      }

      .wm-receipt {
        display: grid;
        grid-template-columns: repeat(4, minmax(0, 1fr));

      }

        min-width: 0;

      }

      .wm-receipt dt {
        color: var(--wm-muted);
        font-size: 0.76rem;
        line-height: 1.25;
      }

      .wm-receipt dd {
        margin: 0.3rem 0 0;
        overflow-wrap: anywhere;
        color: var(--wm-text);
        font-family: "SFMono-Regular", Consolas, ui-monospace, monospace;
        font-size: 0.9rem;
      }

      .wm-evidence {
        margin-block-start: 1.4rem;
      }

        display: block;

        max-width: 100%;

      }

      }

        border-inline-start: 3px solid var(--wm-cyan);
      }

      .wm-duality > div:last-child {

      }

      .wm-duality strong {

        line-height: 1;
      }

        margin-block-start: 0.45rem;

        line-height: 1.35;
      }

      .wm-equation {
        display: block;
        max-width: 100%;
        margin-block: 1.15rem;
        padding: 0.85rem 0;

        border-block: 1px solid var(--wm-rule);
        background: transparent;
        color: var(--wm-text);
        font-family: "SFMono-Regular", Consolas, ui-monospace, monospace;
        font-size: clamp(0.84rem, 1.4vw, 1.05rem);
        line-height: 1.6;
      }

      :where(a, button, input, select, textarea, [tabindex]):focus-visible {
        outline: 3px solid var(--wm-gold) !important;

      }

      .wm-source-links {
        display: grid;
        gap: 0.7rem;

      }

      .wm-source-links a {
        color: var(--wm-cyan);
        text-underline-offset: 0.2em;
      }

      .wm-route {
        display: flex;
        flex-wrap: wrap;
        align-items: baseline;
        gap: 0.55rem 0.8rem;
        margin-block: 1.4rem 0.25rem;

        border-block: 1px solid var(--wm-rule);
        font-size: 0.86rem;
        line-height: 1.45;
      }

      .wm-route-label {
        color: var(--wm-muted);
        font-family: "SFMono-Regular", Consolas, ui-monospace, monospace;
        font-size: 0.72rem;
        font-weight: 700;

      }

      .wm-route a {
        color: var(--wm-text);

      @media (max-width: 900px) {
        .wm-shell {
          padding-inline: clamp(0.9rem, 4vw, 1.5rem);

        }

          margin-inline: auto;
        }

        .wm-section {

        }

        .wm-receipt {
          grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr));
        }

        .wm-duality {
          grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr);
        }

          font-size: clamp(0.66rem, 2.7vw, 0.82rem);
        }

          padding-inline: 0.18rem;
        }
      }

      @media (max-width: 430px) {
        .wm-receipt {
          grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr);
        }

        .wm-section h2 {
          font-size: clamp(2rem, 12vw, 3.1rem);
        }
      }

          --wm-muted: var(--wm-text) !important;

        }

        .wm-kicker,
        .wm-dev,
        .wm-paper,
        .wm-takeaway,

          border-color: CanvasText;
          color: CanvasText;
        }

          scroll-behavior: auto !important;
          animation-duration: 0.001ms !important;
          animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
          transition-duration: 0.001ms !important;
        }
